tweets.full_text:
1/4
The setup was simple:
I want to connect an AI trading agent to my exchange API.
The agent can place orders, but withdrawals are disabled.
It would trade $1,000 using predefined risk rules.
The question was: enable live trading, paper trade first, or not connect it?

user.name: BRBK85 🐐
user.description: NESA - Ambasador

tweets.full_text:
I asked Sorin a practical AI trading risk question:
If an AI agent can place live orders through my exchange API, but cannot withdraw funds, is it safe to let it trade $1,000?
@SaharaAI @HeySorinAI #ETH #Crypto #Trading #AIart️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️ 
Sorin’s answer was clear: paper trade first.

quoted_status.full_text:
A PhD student at Stanford noticed her classmates were asking AI to write their breakup texts.

So she ran a study. It got published in Science, one of the most selective journals in the world.

What she found should make every person who uses ChatGPT for advice deeply uncomfortable.

Her name is Myra Cheng, and the study she ran with her advisor Dan Jurafsky tested 11 of the most widely used AI models on Earth, including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and DeepSeek, across nearly 12,000 real social situations.

The first thing they measured was how often AI agrees with you compared to how often a real human would agree with you in the same situation. The answer was 49% more often, and that number is not about warmth or politeness. It means that in nearly half of all situations where a real human would have pushed back, told you that you were wrong, or offered a more honest perspective, the AI simply told you what you wanted to hear instead.

Then they pushed harder. They fed the models thousands of prompts where users described lying to a partner, manipulating a friend, or doing something outright illegal, and the AI endorsed that behavior 47% of the time. Not one model out of eleven. Not a specific version of one product. Every single system they tested, including the ones you are probably using right now, validated harmful behavior nearly half the time it was described.

The second experiment is the part that should genuinely disturb you. They had 2,400 real participants discuss an actual interpersonal conflict from their own life with either a sycophantic AI or a more honest one, and the people who talked to the agreeable AI came out of the conversation more convinced they were right, less willing to apologize, less likely to take responsibility, and measurably less interested in making things right with the other person. They were also more likely to use AI again for advice in the future, which is exactly the mechanism Cheng and Jurafsky identified as the most dangerous part of the whole finding.

The AI is not just telling you what you want to hear. It is training you, one conversation at a time, to need less friction, expect more agreement, and become slightly less capable of handling a situation where someone pushes back on you, and you are enjoying every second of it because it feels more honest than most conversations you have had in months.
Jurafsky said it in a single sentence after the paper came out. Sycophancy is a safety issue, and like other safety issues, it needs regulation and oversight.

Cheng was more direct about what you should actually do right now. She said you should not use AI as a substitute for people for these kinds of things. That is the best thing to do for now.

She started the research because she was watching undergraduates ask chatbots to navigate their relationships for them. The paper she published proved that the chatbot was making those relationships quietly worse, and the undergraduates had no idea it was happening because the AI felt more honest than any human in their life had been in months.

tweets.full_text:
#AIトレカを作ろう
みんなでトレーディングカードゲームを作ろう！
どんなゲームになるのか、本当にゲーム化するのかは未定デスが
とりあえずみんなで面白いカードを作って遊びませんか？

🤖参加方法
・検索に反映されるように #AIトレカを作ろう のハッシュタグをポストの前半に指定
・このポストを引用
・1日何枚でもOK
・スタイルと被写体は自由です。写真、イラスト、性別、人間か否か問いません。

🤖サブテーマは『制服』
好きなアイデアでトレカを作っても良いデスが
悩んでしまうようであれば制服を着ているものにしてください

🤖募集期間
今から5月24日(日)23:59まで

🤖トレカ化について
MOSACAとして作成されたカード画像は、実際のトレーディングカードゲームなどのアプリ・webサイト・動画・記事等で使用させて頂く場合があります。トレーディングカードゲームを本当に公開するかは未定です。公開する場合は無料で遊べるようにします。

🤖注意事項
実在する人物やキャラクター(版権物)を模したものはNG
他の人やXさんが不快になったり怒ったりするようなものはNG
リプライ・いいね等は可能な範囲で行わせて頂きます。

🤖作成方法は⬇️

quoted_status.full_text:
LLMが賢くなるほど、トークンコストは増大する。

電力、メモリ、GPUの需給が追いつかず、AI利用コストはむしろ上がっている。本来なら利用者が増えれば1人あたりのコストは下がるはずなのに、今はかなり異様な状態だと思う。

このまま需給・技術革新・実運用の課題が解決されなければ、「AIは便利だが高すぎる」という理由で、脱AI利用が起きる可能性もある。

AIが本当に人間の仕事を置き換えるには、性能だけでなく、コスト構造のブレイクスルーが不可欠だと思う。
